ddls:
CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the client */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the client */,
  "company_size" TEXT /* Company size of the client */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the client account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the client account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client the configuration belongs to */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the configuration is deployed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the configuration (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the configuration was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_environment_id FOREIGN KEY ("environment_id") REFERENCES client_environments ("env_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "param_meaning" TEXT /* Meaning of the parameter */,
  "value_space" TEXT /* Value space of the parameter */,
  "binding_time" TEXT /* Binding time of the parameter */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the parameter */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., integer, string, boolean) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "config_params" (
  "config_param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration parameter */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration the parameter belongs to */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter for the configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the configuration parameter was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the configuration parameter was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_params_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_params_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES parameters ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the configuration */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "config_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history record */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration that was changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the parameter before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the parameter after the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, delete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_history_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "config_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration version */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_config_versions_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_environments" (
  "env_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client environment */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client the environment belongs to */,
  "env_name" TEXT /* Name of the environment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the environment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the environment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the environment was last updated */,
  "server_ip" TEXT /* Server IP address of the environment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("env_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_environments_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "deployment_logs" (
  "deploy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each deployment event */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration being deployed */,
  "env_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the configuration is deployed */,
  "deploy_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was deployed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the deployment (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the deployment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("deploy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_deployment_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_deployment_logs_env_id FOREIGN KEY ("env_id") REFERENCES client_environments ("env_id")
)
